headline: Computer Support Technician | IT Service Desk & Endpoint Troubleshooting | User Training & Client-Centered Support
company_focus: client-centered IT support services that keep students, faculty, and staff productive through reliable service desk operations, workstation support, and user training
job_description: Langara College seeks a full-time Computer Support Technician (JR-4265) to provide client-centered technical support through the Service Desk. The role provides primarily Level 2 and occasionally Level 1 support, handling hardware/software incidents and service requests in an ITSM system while providing regular status updates. Responsibilities include installation, configuration, testing, troubleshooting, repair, and upgrade of PC/Mac workstations, printers, peripherals, and lab systems; supporting computer lab operations; assisting inventory and procurement recommendations; and escalating to specialized IT teams as needed. The role also supports basic server/network operations under supervision, documents standards/procedures, contributes to knowledge base updates, and delivers one-on-one/group user training. Required qualifications include a two-year computing diploma (or equivalent combination), two years of technical support experience, and strong communication, troubleshooting, prioritization, and teamwork skills. ITIL Foundation is required; A+, Microsoft, and Mac support certifications are assets.
proximity_note: Living just 10 minutes from the main campus, I am highly motivated to contribute to a vital community institution.

experience.summary: Providing on-site and remote technical support to seniors, families, and community members for Windows/macOS systems, home networking, and mobile device troubleshooting.
experience.executive_summary: Established a long-running freelance IT support practice, building strong customer relationships through reliable, patient technical assistance across diverse systems and environments.
experience.business_impact: Built a reputation-based practice over 10+ years, demonstrating consistent technical problem-solving, user education, and professional customer service.
bullets: Install and configure operating systems, perform hardware upgrades, diagnose and resolve system failures, and remove malware and optimize system performance for end users.
Set up and troubleshoot home routers, Wi-Fi networks, printers, and peripherals; establish backup solutions and educate users on digital hygiene and device maintenance.
Deliver clear technical explanations using non-technical language; maintain professional support standards and customer satisfaction.
Created custom 'How-To' guides for non-technical users to reduce recurring support requests.

experience.summary: Served as the sole technical resource for a small assistive-device ecommerce business (~5 people), handling web development, IT support, security hardening, social media management, and accessibility advocacy.
experience.executive_summary: Sole technical resource for an accessibility-focused small business, responsible for all IT, web, security, and social media operations.
experience.business_impact: Improved the organization's security posture from plaintext password storage to managed credentials, deployed anti-malware tools, and maintained the WooCommerce storefront.
experience.actual_scope_note: Originally hired as web developer; role expanded organically to cover IT support, security hardening, social media (9+ platforms), policy writing, open-source tool migration, and EVAC+ chair accessibility advocacy. Security work was self-directed using LLM guidance prior to formal Lighthouse Labs training.
bullets: Managed WooCommerce e-commerce storefront, product listings, and WordPress site maintenance for assistive-device business.
Migrated organization from plaintext password storage to Bitwarden password manager; deployed Malwarebytes anti-malware across devices.
Configured Windows firewall rules on business endpoints; set up multi-factor authentication on business accounts.
Conducted security reviews of website, business operations, and social media accounts using LLM-guided checklists (pre-formal training).
Led open-source migration initiative: replaced Chrome with Waterfox/Brave, MS Office with LibreOffice, Evernote with Obsidian.
Managed social media presence across 9+ platforms (LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, Pinterest, Alignable, Bark, YouTube, Amazon).
Set up remote access (RustDesk, RealVNC) for technical support of owner's devices; performed hardware upgrades including RAM and Linux installations on secondary laptops.
Created business policies (shipping, returns, privacy, cookies) and training materials for team members.
Coordinated EVAC+ chair accessibility advocacy, including building audits and promotional outreach to Vancouver buildings.

experience.employment_type: Part-time (during studies), transitioning to more hours post-studies
experience.summary: Worked in a high-volume university environment, interacting daily with a diverse student and faculty population. Navigated unionized workplace structures (UNITE HERE Local 40) directly analogous to Langara's institutional environments.
experience.executive_summary: Long-tenure food service cashier in a unionized university dining hall, demonstrating resilience through extremely challenging working conditions concurrent with full-time studies and multiple health issues.
experience.business_impact: Maintained reliable service across 6 years in a high-stress, high-volume campus food service environment.

departure_context.reason: Role ended due to cumulative health issues (physical and mental). Had taken medical leave twice during SFU studies. Experienced burnout from overwork (24-32 hours/week + 3-4 classes/semester with no summer breaks). Also experienced gender dysphoria from dress code requirements (masculine clothing, hairnet, no makeup/jewelry) and constant misgendering due to voice.
departure_context.working_conditions: Frequently worked graveyard shifts as the sole cashier, responsible for the entire dining hall solo. Regularly dealt with 200-400 intoxicated students at 3am attempting to steal food, with only a grill cook and dishwasher on shift. Often denied legally required breaks because no one could cover the register. Had WorkSafeBC involvement at some point.
departure_context.health_impact: The combination of overwork, hostile working conditions, gender dysphoria, and managing multiple health issues (physical and mental) while studying full-time contributed to crashing after 60 credits at SFU.

bullets: Processed sales transactions and provided customer service in a high-volume university buffet dining hall serving hundreds of students per shift.
Worked graveyard shifts as sole cashier, managing facility operations and food security independently.
Operated within a UNITE HERE Local 40 unionized environment, gaining firsthand experience with collective agreements and union workplace culture.
Coordinated duties with kitchen staff in a fast-paced, high-pressure food service setting.
experience.relevance_notes: While not directly relevant to cybersecurity, this role provides: (1) evidence of 6 years of consistent employment demonstrating reliability, (2) unionized work experience valuable for union-role applications (UNITE HERE Local 40), (3) customer service and high-pressure environment skills, (4) personal connection to WorkSafeBC (useful in applications to that organization), (5) context for career timeline completeness. Best mentioned briefly on resumes or highlighted in cover letters for union-based roles.

experience.summary: Board member (elected Dec 2025) supporting open-source education, right-to-repair advocacy, and sustainable technology practices for a 2,200+ member community.
experience.executive_summary: Board member of one of Canada's largest Linux user groups, contributing to strategic planning for 2026 expansion and community event organization.
experience.business_impact: Helping organize educational sessions and advocate practices that keep hardware in use longer and protect user privacy.
bullets: Elected to board of directors at December 2025 AGM; attending monthly board meetings and contributing to 2026 strategic planning.
Organizing and supporting community meetups on Linux, privacy, and sustainable hardware reuse.
Advocating right-to-repair principles and contributing to open-source education initiatives.
Promoting privacy-first tooling and secure operating practices across community members.

custom_cover_letter_body:
I am writing to express my strong interest in the Computer Support Technician position at Langara College. Living less than a 10-minute walk from the main campus, I have long admired Langara’s role as a vital community institution. I am eager to bring my decade of technical troubleshooting, system administration, and client-centered customer service experience to your IT department to help keep students, faculty, and staff productive during critical academic periods.

Throughout my career, I have developed a strong foundation in providing patient, empathetic support across diverse environments. As a Freelance IT Support Technician and the primary IT resource for Accessible Places, I honed my ability to resolve hardware, software, and network issues across Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. I pride myself on translating complex technical concepts into clear, accessible language for users of all technical backgrounds. Furthermore, my time working at Simon Fraser University provided me with deep experience navigating a high-volume, unionized academic environment, equipping me with the institutional awareness necessary to thrive at Langara.

Technically, I am well-prepared to handle the dynamic demands of a college service desk. My CompTIA Security+ certification ensures that I approach endpoint management, access controls, and network troubleshooting with a security-first mindset. Beyond basic troubleshooting, I actively leverage Python and Bash scripting to automate routine maintenance tasks, and I frequently create comprehensive user documentation to reduce recurring support requests.

I am highly motivated to contribute my technical expertise and user-focused approach to the Langara community. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my skills align with your team's needs. Thank you for your time and consideration.
